channel.title: Devops on DevOpsTales
channel.description: Recent content in Devops on DevOpsTales

item.title: Nginx, F5 Acquisition, and the Rise of Angie: Why You Should Consider Switching
item.description: If you’ve been working with web servers and reverse proxies for the past decade, chances are you’ve used nginx. It’s been the backbone of countless web infrastructures, powering everything from small personal blogs to some of the busiest websites on the planet. But recent developments in the nginx ecosystem have led to an interesting fork called Angie — and it might be worth your attention. In this post, I’ll walk through the history of nginx, what happened after the F5 acquisition, why Angie was created, and why you might want to consider making the switch.

item.title: GitHub Actions: Basics
item.description: In this post I will show you how you can use GitHub Actions for CI/CD and pass artifacts between jobs.

item.title: Github Self-Hosted Runners on Kubernetes with Actions Runner Controller
item.description: In this post I will show you how you can set up self-hosted GitHub action runner in Kubernetes with Actions Runner Controller.

item.title: GitLab CI: How to Build Docker Images in Kubernetes
item.description: One of the most common use case is to build a Docker image with Gitlab. In a previous post we used dedicated docker runners for this job. But howe can we build images in a Kubernetes runner? In this post we well se this.

item.title: GitLab CI: How to Build Docker Images in Docker
item.description: One of the most common use case is to build a Docker image with Gitlab. In this post I will show you how to set up Docker builds in CI.

item.title: GitLab CI: artifacts management
item.description: In this post I will show you how you can pass artifacts between in gitlab CI.

item.title: Introduction to GitLab CI/CD
item.description: In this post I will show you the basics of using gitlab as a Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployments (CI/CD) tool.

item.title: What is DevOps?
item.description: This blog post is the first post of my new series where we will learn the use of the most popular CI/CD tools. But first we will understand tha philosophy behind this tools.
